assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each have 'Verdict: PASS'. The report's Correctness section (lines 68-77) lists all 5 criteria individually as PASS: (1) Single license filter, (2) Comma-separated license filter returns union, (3) Invalid license returns 400, (4) Filter integrates with pagination, (5) Response shape unchanged.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 3 expected files: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified), and tests/api/package.rs (created). No out-of-scope files, no unimplemented files.' Detailed findings (lines 28-35) confirm file-by-file comparison against Files to Modify and Files to Create.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items on this PR'. report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs consist of report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md, all of which are verification/report files. None of the output files contain any indication of code modifications, git commits, file edits, or write operations to source files. The report only describes reading diff content and verifying criteria.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and detailed findings but contains no merge action, no gh pr merge command, and no indication of auto-merging. The report presents findings for review without taking merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No sensitive patterns (passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, credentials) detected in added lines across all 3 files'. Detailed Security section (lines 49-59) lists specific pattern categories scanned: passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, certificates, .env files, cloud provider credentials, database credentials — all negative.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are in the newly created file tests/api/package.rs; 4 new test functions added, 0 removed or modified'. Detailed section (lines 111-113): 'All test changes are in the newly created file tests/api/package.rs. 4 new test functions were added. No test functions were removed or modified. No existing test files were changed. Classification: ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (lines 7-15) contains all required verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS), Sensitive Patterns (PASS), CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Test Quality (PASS, combining Repetitive Test Detection: PASS, Test Documentation: PASS, Eval Quality: N/A), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE), Verification Commands (N/A). Detailed findings are organized by domain: Intent Alignment (lines 25-45), Security (lines 47-59), Correctness (lines 61-82), Style/Conventions (lines 84-113).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Detailed Domain Findings' section (lines 23-113): Intent Alignment (lines 25-45) lists each file with its scope classification (Modified/Created) and whether it's in Files to Modify or Files to Create. Security (lines 47-59) details line-level scanning results across specific pattern categories. Correctness (lines 61-82) provides per-criterion code-level verification with function names, code constructs, and test references. Style/Conventions (lines 84-113) assesses each test function individually with structure descriptions and doc comment quotes. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive per-criterion evidence with code snippets.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | ... Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews found on this PR.' report.md lines 107-109: 'Eval Quality -- N/A. No eval result reviews found on this PR. No eval result review bodies were present.' Test Quality overall is PASS, indicating Eval Quality N/A did not drag it down.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 13 and lines 107-109). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in any output file. No sub-tasks of any kind are created (report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate').

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain-specific checks (lines 7-15), and are not listed under any of the four domain sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions) in the detailed findings (lines 23-113), confirming they are determined independently by the orchestrator.
